checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=540557620.07 (2009-01-31); AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=868383287.83 (2010-01-30); Revenues=7181189247.85 (FY2008 end 2009-01-31); Revenues=6833452360.01 (FY2009 end 2010-01-30)
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=1103735320.73 (2014-02-01); Revenues=9022143515.58 (FY2014 end 2015-01-31); AR/Rev ratio stays in a 10-13% band across FY2010-FY2015 rather than trending upward

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=756081183.35 (2009-01-31), InventoryNet=587871410.34 (2010-01-30), InventoryNet=846636954.69 (2014-02-01), InventoryNet=750016061.68 (2015-01-31) - fluctuates without sustained divergence from revenue growth

CostOfGoodsSold=3472170202.61 (FY2008) i.e. 48.3% of Revenues=7181189247.85 (FY2008); CostOfGoodsSold=4487279784.7 (FY2014) i.e. 49.7% of Revenues=9022143515.58 (FY2014) - gradual, not abrupt, margin compression

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=2200502566.28 (FY2010) vs NetIncomeLoss=1497031202.4 (FY2011) vs NetIncomeLoss=746177900.81 (FY2012) - a 66% peak-to-trough decline over two years, indicating earnings are volatile, not smoothed

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=17927642.96 (2011-01-29) declining to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=6481307.79 (2012-01-28), a 64% drop, while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=991211737.1 (2012-01-28) fell only about 11% from AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=1118119297.1 (2011-01-29)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-Q filing_date 2006-09-07; NT 10-Q/A filing_date 2006-09-28; NT 10-Q filing_date 2006-12-06; NT 10-K filing_date 2007-03-29; NT 10-Q filing_date 2007-06-08; 10-K/A filing_date 2007-07-13; 10-Q/A filing_date 2007-07-02

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data points span from FY2007 (period_type annual, end 2008-02-02) through 2015-05-02 across Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, Assets, Liabilities, and cash flow statement concepts

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts receivable fell 64% (AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=17927642.96 (2011-01-29) to 6481307.79 (2012-01-28)) while receivables declined only ~11%, a potential reserve-release signal, though the disclosed series ends at that point

Filing chronology shows a cluster of NT 10-Q/NT 10-K notifications and 10-K/A, 10-Q/A amendments in 2006-2007, indicating historical governance/late-filing risk, though this predates the 2009-2015 financial data reviewed

Otherwise the company shows favorable quality indicators: operating cash flow consistently and materially exceeds net income across all years, intangible assets are amortizing down (not growing), goodwill is flat, and earnings show genuine multi-year volatility rather than artificial smoothing

One large AR/AP/inventory balance sheet jump occurred in fiscal 2010, most consistent with adoption of consolidation accounting for off-balance-sheet financing vehicles (e.g., private-label credit card receivables) rather than manipulation, given AP grew in tandem
